title: Multi-Stage Competitor Ad Parser Pipeline
purpose: Replace monolithic parser with staged pipeline for better noise filtering, debuggability, and evidence tracking.
design_intent: Each stage has isolated responsibility and testable outputs. LLM receives pre-filtered content. Evidence chain is explicit per-product.
change_impact: Major refactor of lib/competitor-intel/crawler/request-handler.ts and lib/competitor-ad-parser/. Backward compatible via adapter layer.

current_issues: Single monolithic function (~300 lines) mixes structure, noise filtering, LLM, and validation
LLM reads 90%+ noise for SharePlan posts (sidebar, nav, license info)
Rule-based parsing fails for 50% of posts - bloated marketing, implicit names, affiliate posts
No post type classification - affiliate/promo/comparison posts treated as direct offers
No per-product confidence breakdown - single aggregate score
Debugging 'why this product?' requires tracing through 5+ decision points
No mechanism to skip stages (e.g., high-confidence Cheerio skip LLM)
No deduplication - redundant scraping of similar posts
staged_benefits: Stage 0 isolates title/body HTML elements, discards platform chrome
Stage 1 classifies post type BEFORE extraction (direct, affiliate, promo, comparison)
Noise filtering happens BEFORE LLM (cleaner context)
Per-product evidence chain (which stage produced which field)
Deduplication via embedding similarity prevents redundant scraping
LLM resolves implicit names (title → body) after classifier confirms post type
rationale.critical_insight: Half of posts require high-dimensional thinking. Bloated marketing titles, affiliate referrals, promo codes, implicit naming. Rule-based Cheerio cannot extract product info from these. Classifier-first architecture required.

stages.name: HTML Locate
stages.critical: Downstream efficiency depends on clean input. Sidebar/nav are static noise.

stages.name: Post Type Classify
stages.responsibility: LLM classify: direct_offer | affiliate | promo_code | comparison
stages.critical: Affiliate/promo posts need different handling. No product extraction for affiliates.

stages.name: Noise Filtering
stages.responsibility: Remove license/footer patterns, validate signal strength

stages.name: LLM Extraction
stages.responsibility: Resolve implicit names (title→body), extract products with evidence
stages.critical: LLM handles bloated marketing titles, implicit naming. Cheerio cannot.

stages.name: Deduplication
stages.responsibility: Check embedding similarity, skip redundant posts
stages.skip_condition: First run only - dedup on subsequent runs

lib/competitor-intel/pipeline/: stage0-locate/index.ts         - HTML element isolation, chrome removal
stage2-filter/index.ts         - Cheerio extraction (step_0) + noise removal + signal scoring
stage3-llm/index.ts            - LLM text extraction (skip if signalScore >= 0.85)

trafilatura.rationale: Tested: SharePlan 82,054 → 1,271 chars (98.5% reduction). Prices preserved. Replaces Cheerio selector chain.
trafilatura.impact: Stage 0 output is plain text (titleText + bodyText). Stage 2 does text pattern matching, not DOM parsing. stage-structure-v1.json Cheerio step removed.

jq.rationale: AI-generated jq commands are non-deterministic and untestable. Noise patterns are deterministic regex — TypeScript handles cleanly.
jq.accepted_use: Dev/debug tooling only: inspect pipeline JSON outputs manually

zod.rationale: Already installed (zod@4.3.6). Stage 3: validates LLM JSON output schema. Stage 4: cross-pair price/duration plausibility check (Pain 4 fix). Zero new dependency.

outlines.fallback: Ollama raw JSON output + Zod validation

duckdb.possible_use: Analytics queries on competitor-ads.db, NOT pipeline automation

backward_compatibility.migration_path: request-handler.ts calls orchestrator.run() instead of direct parsePpomppuPost()

implementation_sequence.name: Stage 2 - Filter (Cheerio Extract + Noise Remove)
acceptance: bodyText split into text blocks (no DOM parsing)
Affiliate/promo posts return early with skip=true
Noise patterns applied to text lines

implementation_sequence.name: Stage 3 - LLM Extract
acceptance: LLM receives filtered content (not raw HTML)
Implicit name resolution uses title from stage0Output

implementation_sequence.name: Stage 4 - Merge
acceptance: Cheerio vs LLM vote logic isolated

acceptance_criteria: Each stage has isolated module with typed input/output
Stage 2 noise filtering reduces LLM context by 50%+ for SharePlan posts
Stage 3 skip condition works (high Cheerio confidence skips LLM)
Stage 4 merge produces source attribution per-product
Stage 5 evidence chain links products to excerpts
Orchestrator runs full pipeline in <5s per post
4 validation posts produce same or better results than monolithic

risk_register.mitigation: Stage 2 preserves price-bearing lines; stage 4 validation catches missing products

risk_register.risk: Stage ordering wrong for certain post types
risk_register.mitigation: Orchestrator allows stage skip conditions; validation posts cover edge cases
Stage 5 asserts evidence per-product; fail-closed on missing evidence
Adapter preserves exact output type; integration tests cover request-handler
